description: Large herbivorous sea turtle often seen grazing on seagrass in calm bays and along reef slopes.

Has a broad, flattened, oval-shaped body with triangular tail fin, a reduced, dulled snout and a small mouth containing bristle-like teeth. The dorsal fin contains 14 spines and 19–21 soft rays, and the anal fin has 3 spines and 20–21 soft rays. The dorsal and anal fins both dangle behind the body. This species attains a maximum total length of 45 cm (18 in) and weight of 1,600 g (56 oz). Males may be larger than females.
distinctive_features: Blue with yellow rims on scales.
abundance: Common in Florida, Bahamas, Gulf of Mexico, Bermuda.
habitat_behavior: Swim slowly in the vicinity of sea whips, sea fans, and corals.
diver_reaction: Wary, tend to keep their distance.

description: Large gray disk-shaped angelfish with a steep forehead and flowing dorsal and anal fins. Adults look mostly gray underwater with bright yellow pectoral fins and a yellow edge on the tail — easy to spot gliding along outer reef slopes.
distinctive_features: Gray body, yellow pectoral fins and tail margin; disk profile like French angelfish.
abundance: Common on Caribbean and Florida reef slopes.
habitat_behavior: Often seen alone or in pairs over sponge and coral on mid-depth reefs.
diver_reaction: Usually keeps a polite distance; slow approaches work best for photos.

description: Striking black and yellow angelfish common on Caribbean reefs, often seen in pairs near coral heads.
Graceful ray with white spots on a dark back, often gliding over sand flats and reef drop-offs.
Curious schooling squid that flash color patterns while hovering over reefs, especially on night dives.
